observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Prioritize and devote time to create a sanitary landfill considering that a lot has already been purchased for this project. Upon completion of the same, the open dumpsite must be closed. The project for the road opening that would give access to the sanitary landfill must be implemented immediately; and 

Coordinate with the EMB Regional Office to have updates on the status of the Solid Waste Management Plan, if the same has already been approved, and on the possible revisions needed.
observations.managementAction: Initiate the closure of the open dumpsite facility. Segregation policy will be implemented. Lastly, the Management will ensure strict compliance in solid waste management disposal.
observations.reason: The LGU prioritized implementation of COVID-19 PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management construct a sanitary landfill and stop the use of an open controlled dumpsite. Segregation policy must be imposed upon collection of garbage. It is highly suggested that wastes/garbage which are not segregated at source must not be collected by garbage collector;
observations.managementAction: Initiate the closure of the open dumpsite facility. Segregation policy will be implemented. Lastly, the Management will ensure strict compliance in solid waste management disposal.
observations.reason: The Management has just taken the preparatory steps in establishment of Solid Waste Disposal Facility. Nevertheless, a lot has been purchased intended for the sanitary landfill. Closure of the open dumpsite is now in process.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all Municipality’s PPE and submit the corresponding RPCPPE. During the actual count, the type, description, serial number, acquisition date, name/designation of employee accountable, location and condition of the property counted must be indicated in the Inventory Sheet.
We also recommended that Management:
Maintain Stock Cards and Property Cards in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Moreover, PAR must be used to document issuance of PPE to end-users to establish custodianship and assign accountability;
Require the Inventory Committee, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct actual physical count of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Thereafter, they must perform reconciliation of the result of the actual physical count of PPE with the required PPE records to establish correctness and validity of the account balances presented in the financial statements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule of all PPE to properly determine the correctness and validity of the reported depreciation expenses and carrying value of PPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
Municipal Accountant to: i) exert more effort in retracing the beginning book balances of individual bank accounts under TF in order to present a more accurate SLs and BRSs therefor; and ii) prepare supporting schedules for all reconciling items in BRSs especially those due to errors; and
Municipal Treasurer to close the accounts that have become dormant and revert back to the Source Agency or the Bureau of the Treasury, as the case maybe, if the said accounts represent excess of the expenses for the PPAs funded by NGAs and/or other Agencies.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the concerned official to coordinate with the Department of Agriculture (DA) and inquire as to the status of the fund transfer pertinent to the TF accounts – DA – Bottom-up Budgeting (BuB).

We recommended that the Accounting Office:
Prepare and maintain SLs as many as necessary for all the accounts appearing in the financial statements to determine the correctness of account balances at any given period; and
Use the available data in the accounting records as beginning balances of the SLs to facilitate preparation and maintenance thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management facilitate immediately the request for write-off of the aforementioned account balances so that the same will be derecognized in the Financial Statements, subject to the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor all paid DVs for CY 2021 still in their possession; (ii) ensure submission of paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month; and (iii) and avoid voluminous submissions of DVs pertaining to transactions for several months all at once; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs and official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management:
Judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent Economic DF in accordance with the mandate of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020 dated November 4, 2021;
Require the MDC to ensure that the development projects identified in the Annual Development Plan (ADP) are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation; and
Observe prudence on the basis of amounts for realignment by consulting with concerned offices on the balance of unutilized appropriations for PPAs.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe prompt transfer of deposit to TF of current year unutilized portion of LDRRMF (QRF and DRRMF-MOOE);
Require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the following journal entries relevant to transfer of unutilized LDRRMF in case there is no actual cash transfer during the year:
Transfer of the current year unutilized balance without actual cash transfer:
General Fund
Dr. Transfers of Unspent DRRMF to the TF	xxx
Cr. Due to Other Funds				xxx
Trust Fund

Cr. Trust Liabilities-DRRMF		xxx
Entry in the succeeding year for the actual transfer of cash of prior year unutilized balance:
General Fund
Dr. Due to Other Funds			xxx
Cr. Cash in Bank				xxx
Trust Fund
Dr. Cash in Bank			xxx
Cr. Due From Other Funds			xxx
Moreover, SLs for the accounts must be maintained so that annual transfer from the GF to the TF could be monitored and recorded. Likewise, the Accounting Office must exercise diligence in the recording of financial transactions to achieve fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements;
Ensure that the Municipal Accountant make the following adjusting journal entry in the succeeding year for CY 2021 unutilized balance where no transfer was made in the current year:
General Fund
Dr. Prior Period Adjustment		xxx
Cr. Cash in Bank				xxx
Trust Fund
Dr. Cash in Bank			xxx
Cr. Trust Liabilities-DRRMF		xxx
Ensure that only the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE are transferred to the Special TF while the unexpended balance of MF-CO must be made continuing in the GF books until the projects funded therefor are completed.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the concerned official to submit copies of perfected contracts and POs together with the complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after execution for purposes of review thereof and technical evaluation by the COA TAS, as the case may be.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the LDRRM Officer to secure immediately the needed documents to facilitate the timely preparation of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit a copy thereof to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, to facilitate verification thereof.
Likewise, we recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences between the balance of previous years’ unutilized appropriation per LDRRMF Utilization Report, Notes to Financial Statement and Trust Liability – DRRM account;
Maintain individual records per calendar year for the remaining unutilized balance of Special TF for the previous five years; and
Ensure that the LDRRMF Utilization Report include those payable to BIR which are directly attributable to purchase of materials or services, and are appropriated from LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
Direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers;
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee with existing unliquidated cash advance. However, if it is necessary to grant cash advances for special activities, Management must grant the same to the designated Special Disbursing Officer; and
Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees (AO) and be reminded that the Auditor may order the suspension of their salaries, as cited in Section 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-004.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first day of December each year, an assessment roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount collectible at the beginning of the year as basis for the set-up of RPT collectible for the year;
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the following adjusting journal entries:
General Fund books
Correction of recording of discount of RPT
Dr.	Due to LGUs				15,553.51
Cr. 	Prior Period Adjustment			 15,553.51
Correction of set-up of RPT collectible for CY 2021
Dr.	Real Property Tax Receivable		2,664,860.75
Cr. 	Deferred Real Property Tax Receivable	 2,664,860.75
Special Education Fund books
Correction of recording of discount of SET
Dr.	Due to LGUs				38,883.79
Cr. 	Prior Period Adjustment			 38,883.79
Correction of set-up of RPT collectible for CY 2021
Dr.	Special Education Tax Receivable		2,664,860.75
Cr. 	Deferred Special Education Tax Receivable	 2,664,860.75; and
Ensure that the reported amount of Discount on RPT- Basic and SEF represent the apportioned share of the LGU in the discount.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to observe strict adherence to accrual basis of accounting in the recording of financial transactions of the Municipality such that the expenditures and obligations are recorded during the year they are incurred so as to reflect the correct expenses in the financial statement; and
Strictly follow the procurement process prescribed in RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR, and minimize the procurement through reimbursement in order to maintain controls on budgeting and avoid budget deficit.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require all drivers and concerned officials to completely fill out the Driver’s Trip Ticket in accordance with existing regulation for transparency and accountability over the use of such government motor vehicles; and
Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report of LGU vehicles and heavy equipment to the Commission on Audit (thru the Municipal Accountant) for verification and evaluation of the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management prepare the Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation of LGSF-DRRAP and thereafter post the same in the LGU website and in at least three conspicuous places within the locality. Afterwards, notify in writing the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices when these monthly reports were posted on the LGU’s website.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking documents for audit purposes. Henceforth, Municipal Accountant must ensure complete documentation for every type of transaction, in accordance with applicable rules and regulations, before affixing his/her signature in the DV.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the concerned personnel to facilitate reconciliation of the unaccounted balance by conducting complete annual physical count of PPE and submit to the Auditor the RPCPPE for verification in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant must properly maintain Stock Cards, Property Cards, SLC and PPELC and conduct periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records to come up with accurate reports.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE has already issued Office Order for the Designation of officer for the purpose of ensuring that PPE will be accounted for and recorded for submission to COA.

Management committed to comply with audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
Physical Inventory was not conducted due to COVID-19 situation.

Priorities were given more on the implementation of COVID-19 PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the:

Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the variance in the Cash in Bank account between book and bank records pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 and prepare Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) per bank account; and

Concerned official to coordinate with the Department of Agriculture and inquire as to the status of the fund transfer pertinent to the Account Trust Fund – DA – BUB.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to review their records as basis for splitting the total Cash in bank balance in TF books into balances for Regular TF and Special Trust Fund respectively.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to prepare Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in TF due to non-maintenance of separate Cash in bank balances of Regular TF and Special Trust Fund.

Strictly follow the procurement process prescribed in RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR, and minimize the procurement through reimbursement in order to maintain controls on budgeting and avoid budget deficit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to trace all available records and initiate inquiries with the former Municipal Accountant of the information of the actual dormant accounts and prepare quarterly aging thereof; and

Facilitate the request for write-off of the dormant accounts so that the same will be derecognized in the Financial Statements and conform with the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Management has tried all necessary means to trace available records, but the Accounting Office did not find any information on the actual dormant accounts.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records or information

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary correcting entry to adjust the balance of the Due to LGU account. Moreover, the concerned official must gather information relevant thereof so that subsidiary ledger for the account will be maintained. Lastly, the Municipal Accounting Office must exercise diligence in the recording of financial transactions to achieve fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant conduct a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled accounts.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.
observations.managementAction: Management has tried all necessary means to trace available records, but the Accounting Office did not find any information on the cause of unreconciled reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records or information

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month;

Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs, Official Receipts including the corresponding reports of each month, on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and

Submit immediately the aforementioned lacking DVs, duplicate copy of checks and their supporting documents to avoid audit suspension thereof.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to adhere to the required activities and timelines in the planning and budgeting of Special Education Fund (SEF). SEF budget must be prepared and must conform with the schedule of activities cited in Section 5.3 of DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular No. 2017-01. Moreover, it was recommended that the LSB ensure optimum utilization of SEF to address the budgetary needs of the beneficiary public schools.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Lack of conduct of required monthly meetings of LSB contributed to non-adherence to required activities and timelines in the planning and budgeting of SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned official to comply with the mandatory minimum allotment for GAD which is five percent of the Agency’s total budget, includes both IRA and other local income sources;

Require the GAD Focal Person to observe compliance with the submission of the copy of GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office on or before March of the preceding year for appropriate review and approval. DILG-approved GPB must include a certificate of approval from the DILG Regional Office and must be incorporated in the year’s annual budget to be enacted by the Sanggunian. Also, GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 must be submitted immediately to the Provincial Government for the required review and consolidation; and

Ensure that implementation of GAD PPAs must be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the unremitted shares of the Province and Barangays out of its collections from real property and special education taxes. Regular monitoring and coordination with the concerned LGUs must be observed to avoid accumulation of the balances. Moreover, records for the share of specific Barangay in RPT collections must be maintained so that remittance to the latter will be expediently facilitated;

Require the Municipal Accountant to come up with a reconciliation of the variance between the balance of RPT and SET Receivables; and

Intensify its tax collection scheme by sending regular billings/notice to taxpayers and making personal follow-ups constantly on the delinquent ones.
observations.managementAction:
Management is yet to initiate the reconciliation with Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) in order to establish the exact amount to be remitted by the LGU to their office.

Untraceable records for the Reconciliation

The LGU partially remitted one million pesos and will remit the remaining amount after the PTO reconcile the amount to be remitted by the LGU. Management committed to further employ the necessary means for the reconciliation.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately balance of undeposited collections and to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to collections and deposits as required under Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and

Require the Collecting Officers to turnover or remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer. The Liquidating Officer must verify the unused accountable forms in the possession of collecting clerks upon remittance to see to it that all collections are remitted daily and intact. The new booklet of accountable forms must only be issued upon full remittance and consumption thereof to prevent the collecting clerks from retaining huge amount of collections in their custody.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer will deposit the undeposited amount incurred in her tenure.

Require all drivers and concerned officials to completely fill out the Driver’s Trip Ticket in accordance with existing regulation for transparency and accountability over the use of such government motor vehicles;

Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report of LGU vehicles and heavy equipment to the Commission on Audit (thru Municipal Accountant) for verification and evaluation of the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct:

The concerned official to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the aforecited 15 cancelled checks for validation and henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “cancelled” clearly indicated therein; and

The Treasury Office to exercise caution in the preparation of checks to avoid possible cancellation thereof.

We recommended that Management ensure that Supplemental Annual Investment Programs (AIP) be prepared for future government grants requiring compliance with the regular budget process. Moreover, it was recommended that Management be more vigilant in observing proper planning and budgeting prior to utilization of government fund.

We recommended that the Sanggunian enact/pass supplemental appropriation ordinance for future budget realignment clearly indicating the sources of funds available for appropriation, certified under oath by the local treasurer and local accountant and attested by the local chief executive, and the various items of appropriations affected and the reasons for the change. Likewise, Management was reminded that the purpose of fund realignment was to enable the LGU to undertake critical, urgent and appropriate disaster response aid and measures to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

We recommended that Management:

Submit immediately the documents such as authority to physically report for work during ECQ and DTRs to the Office of the Auditor to avoid audit suspension of the aforementioned transaction; and

Require the personnel who received the COVID-19 hazard pay but was not yet employed by the LGU during ECQ, to refund the same, and personnel in the Municipal Health Office who received the same benefit but were already entitled to Hazard Pay under existing laws, issuances, rules, and regulations, to refund the excess, to avoid audit disallowance thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Require the Budget/Accounting Office to prepare and submit to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, and to the Office of the Auditor the report on the receipt and utilization of future cash donations; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entry to adjust the balances of the affected accounts. Moreover, it is recommended that the concerned official promptly monitor the transactions in the trust fund books most especially fund transfers and diligently apply relevant accounting treatment.

We recommended that Management:

Require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually. It must present the 30 percent allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded. The same must also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years;

Ensure that updated or supplemental LDRRMFIP be prepared for any future changes or modification in the utilization of LDRRMF; and

Require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMF utilization report in accordance with the required format cited in COA Circular 2012-002 and promptly submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of the month for monitoring and evaluation of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Timely submission of the report cannot be made due to the voluminous transactions of the Agency

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect adjusting entries pertinent to the overstatement of the inventory accounts and understatement of expenses for CY 2019. We reminded the Municipal Accountant to improve the controls with regard to the monitoring of accounting policies applied per transactions.
observations.managementAction: The said balances were derecognized in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

Management exert effort and devote time to comply with the physical inventory taking of its Agency’s assets. The RPCPPE must be submitted to the Auditor and to the Local Chief Executive; and

Management direct the General Services Officer (GSO)/Municipal Treasurer to maintain property records and the Municipal Accountant to prepare ledger cards for each class of PPE.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE has already issued Office Order for the Designation of officer for the purpose of ensuring that PPE will be accounted for and recorded for submission to COA.

Property records were not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant already  complied with the preparation of ledger cards for each class of PPE.
observations.reason:
Physical Inventory was not conducted due to COVID-19 situation.

Due to the application of Alternative Work Arrangements, the concerned office was not able to maintain the required property records and ledger cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

Management direct the Treasury Department to immediately collect from the erring taxpayers or accountable constituents their liabilities during CY 2019 arising from the unpaid fees, permits or charges. The Treasury Department must send letters to these people and impose appropriate penalties, if possible; and

The Treasury Department be more vigilant in the collection of the local income sources. There must be an established and strong internal control pertinent to the collection scheme of the Agency which must be headed by the Municipal Treasurer. It is reminded that the annual appropriation of the Agency contains figures of the estimated local income sources. Inability to reach the said estimated results is a deprivation of the corresponding benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
Taxpayers were reminded of their accountabilities through personal follow-ups and demand letters.

Internal control of the Treasury Department was improved, and collection scheme was enhanced.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Submit immediately the accounts covering the period July to December 2019 for evaluation and conduct of post-audit;

Direct its officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the regulation on the effective and efficient rendition of accounts and financial reports for the timely verification of accounts and conduct of post-audit activities; and

Devote some time to address the issue on the delayed/non-submission of accounts since the same has been a repeated audit finding for several years now.
observations.managementAction:
Accounts were immediately submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

Officials and employees were reminded of their responsibilities in the effective and efficient rendition of accounts.
observations.reason: Timely rendition of accounts was not complied due to alternative work arrangements during pandemic. The concerned offices were precluded from accomplishing required reports due to absence of proper signatories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the prescribed accounting guidelines and control procedures on the receipt, issuance and storage/custodianship of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: The offices will be are reminded to strictly observe the prescribed accounting guidelines and control procedures on the receipt, issuance and storage/ custodianship of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: The prescribed accounting guidelines and control procedures on the receipt, issuance and storage/custodianship of supplies and materials was not followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all undeposited collections as of year-end after reconciling records with the Municipal Accountant. The practice of accumulation of collections before they are deposited must be stopped. Also, we recommended to verify and effect adjustments, if necessary, to the balance of the Cash-Local Treasury account under the Trust Fund.

The treasurer cannot deposit the total amount because most of the undeposited collection were incurred during the incumbency of the former treasurer.

The Municipal Treasurer will deposit the undeposited amount incurred in her tenure.
observations.reason: Collections referred to in the audit observation remained undeposited. Collections are still accumulated before they are deposited. Further, the Municipal Accountant did not verify and effect necessary adjustments of the balance of the Cash-Local Treasury Account in the trust fund due to limitations during this pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect correcting entries to include the actual RPT Receivables and Deferred Revenues in the Statement of Financial Position which were omitted during CY 2019. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant was reminded to observe caution and prudence in the presentation and preparation of accounting reports. Henceforth, ensure that receivable accounts are valued at the actual amount of tax due and collectible to attain fair presentation of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant effected correcting entry to include the actual RPT and SET receivables in the FS.

observations.recommendation:

Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the unremitted shares of the provincial government, barangay governments and the provincial school board out of it collections for RPT and SET;

The Municipal Treasurer conduct a meeting with all Barangays to discuss the policy in releasing their respective shares in the RPTs; and

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly coordinate with the provincial government and provincial school board with respect to the release of the said shares to avoid accumulation of the same.

Moreover, the recipients did not initiate to claim for their shares.

Lack of coordination with the concerned Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality establish a reasonable amount for Petty Cash Fund and use the same for non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses. The practice of reimbursing expenditures previously shouldered by an employee must be stopped. Direct payment to supplier must be done through issuance of check or through the petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant initiated to retrieve all necessary documents and records in the prior years for the reconciliation of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality establish a reasonable amount for Petty Cash Fund and use the same for non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses. The practice of reimbursing expenditures previously shouldered by an employee must be stopped. Direct payment to supplier must be done through issuance of check or through the petty cash fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and trace the transactions pertinent to the Other Prepayments account. Adjustments must be effected immediately. We also recommended to improve the controls with regard to the monitoring of accounting transactions specifically on the adjusting entries at the statement date.
observations.managementAction: Transactions pertinent to the Other Prepayments account were reviewed and the necessary adjustments were effected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

Management direct the MDRRMO to strictly observe the submission of the LDRRMF utilization report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Office of the Auditor;

Management direct the concerned officials to transfer the unutilized balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to a special trust fund account immediately; and

The LDRRMFIP be prepared annually which must present the 30 per cent allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded.

We recommended that:

The Inventory Committee complete the inventory count especially of those which were excluded in the inventory taking during CY 2018. The said report of these uninspected assets must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor;

Management consider creating a General Service Officer position for a convenient and efficient maintenance of records on all properties of the Agency; and 

The Municipal Accountant maintain ledger cards of all properties. This must be reconciled with stock/property cards maintained by the GSO or Municipal Treasurer.

The Municipal Accountant assigned one her staff to maintain ledger cards of all properties.

Management appointed the Municipal Treasurer as in charge in maintaining records on all properties of the LGU.
observations.reason:
Lack of supervision and monitoring.

The GSO position was not created due to other concerns of the LGU.

Too much workload of the Municipal Accountant deprived her in complying with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant come up with a reconciliation of the unreconciled amount totaling ₱12.44 million between the book and bank balance. The Municipal Accountant must effect the necessary adjustments in the books after the reconciliation has been done.  The Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) must be submitted together with the copies of Bank Statements and paid checks, to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 20th day of the following month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.  Moreover, the Audit Team also suggests additional manpower in the Accounting Department to help perform accounting duties so that the Municipal Accountant can devote time to prepare the bank reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared bank reconciliation statements and will be ready for submission to COA.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to prepare Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements due to workload imposed in her department considering there exists lack of manpower therein.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer submit to Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and

The Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
require the concerned personnel to facilitate reconciliation of the unaccounted
balance by conducting complete annual physical count of PPE and submit to the
Auditor the RPCPPE for verification in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual
on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant must properly maintain
Stock Cards, Property Cards, SLC and PPELC and conduct periodic reconciliation
of property and accounting records to come up with accurate reports.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the:

a) Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the variance in the Cash
in Bank account between book and bank records pursuant to COA
Circular No. 96-011 and prepare Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) per bank
account; and
b) Concerned official to coordinate with the Department of Agriculture and
inquire as to the status of the fund transfer pertinent to the Account Trust
Fund – DA – BUB.

We recommended that Management:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to observe strict adherence to accrual
basis of accounting in the recording of financial transactions of the
Municipality. Accrual basis mandates that transactions and other events
are recognized in the Financial Statements when they occur and not only
when cash or its equivalent is received or paid; and
b) Strictly adhere to Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445 to avoid possible shortage of
budget or appropriation.

We recommended that Management:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to trace all available records and
initiate inquiries with the former Municipal Accountant of the information
of the actual dormant accounts and prepare quarterly aging thereof; and
b) Facilitate the request for write-off of the dormant accounts so that the
same will be derecognized in the Financial Statements and conform with
the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to effect
necessary correcting entry to adjust the balance of the Due to LGU account.
Moreover, the concerned official must gather information relevant thereof so that
subsidiary ledger for the account will be maintained. Lastly, the Municipal
Accounting Office must exercise diligence in the recording of financial transactions to
achieve fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant conduct a
detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled accounts.
Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to
present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in
recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the
balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.

We recommended that Management:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor
the paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all
the DVs, Official Receipts including the corresponding reports of each
month, on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and
c) Submit immediately the aforementioned lacking DVs, duplicate copy of
checks and their supporting documents to avoid audit suspension thereof.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to adhere to the
required activities and timelines in the planning and budgeting of Special Education
Fund (SEF). SEF budget must be prepared and must conform with the schedule of
activities cited in Section 5.3 of DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular No. 2017-01.
Moreover, it was recommended that the LSB ensure optimum utilization of SEF to
address the budgetary needs of the beneficiary public schools.

We recommended that Management:

a) Require the concerned official to comply with the mandatory minimum
allotment for GAD which is five percent of the Agency’s total budget,
includes both IRA and other local income sources;
b) Require the GAD Focal Person to observe compliance with the submission
of the copy of GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office on or
before March of the preceding year for appropriate review and approval.
DILG-approved GPBs must include a certificate of approval from the
DILG Regional Office and must be incorporated in the year’s annual
budget to be enacted by the Sanggunian. Also, GAD Accomplishment
Report for CY 2020 must be submitted immediately to the Provincial
Government for the required review and consolidation; and
c) Ensure that implementation of GAD PPAs must be in accordance with the
approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management:

a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the unremitted
shares of the Province and Barangays out of it collections from real
property and special education taxes. Regular monitoring and
coordination with the concerned LGUs must be observed to avoid
accumulation of the balances. Moreover, records for the share of specific
Barangay in RPT collections must be maintained so that remittance to the
latter will be expediently facilitated;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to come up with a reconciliation of the
variance between the balance of RPT and SET Receivables; and
c) Intensify its tax collection scheme by sending regular billings/notice to
taxpayers and making personal follow-ups constantly on the delinquent
ones.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately balance of
undeposited collections and to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to
collections and deposits as required under Section 32 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b) Require the Collecting Officers to turnover or remit all collections daily and
intact to the Liquidating Officer. The Liquidating Officer must verify the
unused accountable forms in the possession of collecting clerks upon
remittance to see to it that all collections are remitted daily and intact. The
new booklet of accountable forms must only be issued upon full remittance
and consumption thereof to prevent the collecting clerks from retaining
huge amount of collections in their custody

We recommended that Management:

a) Require all drivers and concerned officials to completely fill out the
Driver’s Trip Ticket in accordance with existing regulation for
transparency and accountability over the use of such government motor
vehicles;
b) Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel
Consumption Report of LGU vehicles and heavy equipment to the
Commission on Audit (thru Municipal Accountant) for verification and
evaluation of the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month; and
c) Provide a complete list of serviceable municipal vehicles to ascertain the
reliability of fuel consumption and for validation purposes.

We recommended that Management direct:

a) The concerned official to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor,
the aforecited 15 cancelled checks for validation and henceforth, ensure
that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported
chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “cancelled”
clearly indicated therein; and
b) The Treasury Office to exercise caution in the preparation of checks to
avoid possible cancellation thereof.

We recommended that Management ensure that Supplemental Annual
Investment Programs (AIP) be prepared for future government grants requiring
compliance with the regular budget process. Moreover, it was recommended that
Management be more vigilant in observing proper planning and budgeting prior to
utilization of government fund.

We recommended that the Sanggunian enact/pass supplemental appropriation
ordinance for future budget realignment clearly indicating the sources of funds
available for appropriation, certified under oath by the local treasurer and local
accountant and attested by the local chief executive, and the various items of
appropriations affected and the reasons for the change. Likewise, Management was
reminded that the purpose of fund realignment was to enable the LGU to undertake
critical, urgent and appropriate disaster response aid and measures to curtail and
eliminate the threat of COVID-19

We recommended that Management:

a) Submit immediately the documents such as authority to physically report
for work during ECQ and DTRs to the Office of the Auditor to avoid audit
suspension of the aforementioned transaction; and
b) Require the personnel who received the COVID-19 hazard pay but was not
yet employed by the LGU during ECQ, to refund the same, and personnel
in the Municipal Health Office who received the same benefit but were
already entitled to Hazard Pay under existing laws, issuances, rules, and
regulations, to refund the excess, to avoid audit disallowance thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Require the Budget/Accounting Office to prepare and submit to the
NDRRMC, through the OCD, and to the Office of the Auditor the report
on the receipt and utilization of future cash donations; and
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entry
to adjust the balances of the affected accounts. Moreover, it is
recommended that the concerned official promptly monitor the
transactions in the trust fund books most especially fund transfers and
diligently apply relevant accounting treatment.

We recommended that Management:

a) Require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually. It must
present the 30 percent allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation
for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to
projects and activities to be funded. The same must also include under a
separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the
unexpended LDRRMF of previous years;
b) Ensure that updated or supplemental LDRRMFIP be prepared for any
future changes or modification in the utilization of LDRRMF; and
c) Require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMF utilization report in
accordance with the required format cited in COA Circular 2012-002 and
promptly submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th
day after the end of the month for monitoring and evaluation of the fund.

We recommended that:

The Inventory Committee complete the inventory count especially of those which were excluded in the inventory taking during CY 2018. The said report of these uninspected assets must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor;

Management consider creating a General Service Officer position for a convenient and efficient maintenance of records on all properties of the Agency; and 



The Municipal Accountant maintain ledger cards of all properties. This must be reconciled with stock/property cards maintained by the GSO or Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect adjusting entries pertinent to the overstatement of the inventory accounts and understatement of expenses for CY 2019. We reminded the Municipal Accountant to improve the controls with regard to the monitoring of accounting policies applied per transactions.

We recommended that:
Management exert effort and devote time to comply with the physical inventory taking of its Agency’s assets. The RPCPPE must be submitted to the Auditor and to the Local Chief Executive; and
Management direct the General Services Officer (GSO)/Municipal Treasurer to maintain property records and the Municipal Accountant to prepare ledger cards for each class of PPE.

We recommended that:
Management direct the Treasury Department to immediately collect from the erring taxpayers or accountable constituents their liabilities during CY 2019 arising from the unpaid fees, permits or charges. The Treasury Department must send letters to these people and impose appropriate penalties, if possible; and
The Treasury Department be more vigilant in the collection of the local income sources. There must be an established and strong internal control pertinent to the collection scheme of the Agency which must be headed by the Municipal Treasurer. It is reminded that the annual appropriation of the Agency contains figures of the estimated local income sources. Inability to reach the said estimated results is a deprivation of the corresponding benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that Management:
Submit immediately the accounts covering the period July to December 2019 for evaluation and conduct of post-audit;
Direct its officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the regulation on the effective and efficient rendition of accounts and financial reports for the timely verification of accounts and conduct of post-audit activities; and
Devote some time to address the issue on the delayed/non-submission of accounts since the same has been a repeated audit finding for several years now.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the prescribed accounting guidelines and control procedures on the receipt, issuance and storage/custodianship of supplies and materials.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all undeposited collections as of year-end after reconciling records with the Municipal Accountant. The practice of accumulation of collections before they are deposited must be stopped. Also, we recommended to verify and effect adjustments, if necessary, to the balance of the Cash-Local Treasury account under the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect correcting entries to include the actual RPT Receivables and Deferred Revenues in the Statement of Financial Position which were omitted during CY 2019. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant was reminded to observe caution and prudence in the presentation and preparation of accounting reports. Henceforth, ensure that receivable accounts are valued at the actual amount of tax due and collectible to attain fair presentation of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that:
Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the unremitted shares of the provincial government, barangay governments and the provincial school board out of it collections for RPT and SET;
The Municipal Treasurer conduct a meeting with all Barangays to discuss the policy in releasing their respective shares in the RPTs; and
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly coordinate with the provincial government and provincial school board with respect to the release of the said shares to avoid accumulation of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and reconcile the Due from the Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances. Likewise, ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same so as not to affect the presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipality establish a reasonable amount for Petty Cash Fund and use the same for non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses. The practice of reimbursing expenditures previously shouldered by an employee must be stopped. Direct payment to supplier must be done through issuance of check or through the petty cash fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and trace the transactions pertinent to the Other Prepayments account. Adjustments must be effected immediately. We also recommended to improve the controls with regard to the monitoring of accounting transactions specifically on the adjusting entries at the statement date.

We recommended that Management:
Prioritize and devote time to create a sanitary landfill considering that a lot has already been purchased for this project. Upon completion of the same, the open dumpsite must be closed. The project for the road opening that would give access to the sanitary landfill must be implemented immediately; and
Coordinate with the EMB Regional Office to have updates on the status of the Solid Waste Management Plan, if the same has already been approved, and on the possible revisions needed.

We recommended that:
Management direct the MDRRMO to strictly observe the submission of the LDRRMF utilization report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRM Council and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Office of the Auditor;
Management direct the concerned officials to transfer the unutilized balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to a special trust fund account immediately; and
The LDRRMFIP be prepared annually which must present the 30 per cent allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded.

We recommended to Management that disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) must be done in accordance with the guidelines and accounting procedures set forth in the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to effectively monitor and determine whether or not the funds were utilized for its intended purpose and that expenses incurred were appropriately charged to fund. Disbursement of SEF through fund transfers/donations to schools must be stopped. Moreover, require the beneficiaries of CY 2018 to submit liquidation reports of the fund transfer.

We recommended that Management direct/instruct the Municipal Development Council to strictly include programs and projects chargeable to the 20 per cent Local Development Fund that are specifically embodied in the DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure the attainment of desirable social, economic and developmental outcomes for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that Management:
Create a GAD Focal Point System. This is through the Local Chief Executive signing appropriate issuances appointing LGU Officials to become part of the said GFPS. This must be composed of an Executive Committee and a Technical Working Group. This is lengthily discussed in Section 4a of PCW- DILG –DBM- NEDA Joint Memo Circular No. 2013-01;

Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and DILG Regional Office V for appropriate review and approval. The corresponding Accomplishment Report must also be prepared and submitted; and
Follow the proper way of budgeting in the allocation of 5 per cent for Gender and Development transactions.

We recommended that Management adhere to the above cited implementing laws, rules and regulations pertinent to appropriation/disbursement of government fund for religious purpose. Management must exercise prudence in the disbursement of fund whether the same is in compliance with existing rules and regulations. We also recommend that Management direct the officials concerned to attach all lacking documents of the purchase from Fort’z Samut Sari-Sari Store.

We recommended that Management stop paying for the hotel accommodation of other government agency personnel staying in Monreal, Masbate because the latter are also given hotel allowances as part of their travel expenses. Moreover it is reminded that the Agency exercise caution in the disbursement of fund by adhering to rules and regulations to avoid those Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant, or Unconscionable (IUEEU) expenditures.

We recommended that Management: 

Submit the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor without further demand;

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the applicable provisions in the procurement of drugs and medicines provided under RA 3720 as amended and Executive Order No. 49 and other pertinent laws; and

Instruct the Accounting Office to require adequate documentation on each transaction involving procurement of goods before payment is effected.

We recommended to:

See to it that the 20 per cent Development Fund be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

Implement immediately the thirteen (13) programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance as mentioned in the preceding discussion.

We recommended to send demand letters and/or exhaust all possible legal actions to the former Municipal Treasurer Ms. Belmonte to settle all his unliquidated cash advances. In addition, we advised that the Honorable Municipal Mayor: (a) direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who fail to comply with the requirement; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and send written demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; (c) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances; and (d) take legal actions against previous employees with still unliquidated cash advances who are no longer in the government service.

Also, it is reminded that pursuant to Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 040676 dated June 17, 2004 as amended by CSC Resolution No. 1200103 dated January 12, 2012, the failure of an accountable officer to render an account in full within the periods prescribed and after formal demand shall constitute the administrative offense of Gross Neglect of Duty punishable by dismissal from the service for the first offense. Full liquidation/settlement/payment of the subject cash advance outside the given periods shall constitute the offense of Simple Neglect of Duty punishable by suspension from the service for one (1) month and one (1) day to six (6) months for the first offense, and dismissal from the service for the second offense.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Inventory Committee complete the inventory count especially of those which were excluded in the inventory taking during CY 2018. The said report of these uninspected assets must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor;
Management consider creating a General Service Officer position for a convenient and efficient maintenance of records on all properties of the Agency; and
The Municipal Accountant maintain ledger cards of all properties. This must be reconciled with stock/property cards maintained by the GSO or Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant come up with a reconciliation of the unreconciled amount totaling ₱12.44 million between the book and bank balance. The Municipal Accountant must effect the necessary adjustments in the books after the reconciliation has been done. The Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) must be submitted together with the copies of Bank Statements and paid checks, to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 20th day of the following month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. Moreover, the Audit Team also suggests additional manpower in the Accounting Department to help perform accounting duties so that the Municipal Accountant can devote time to prepare the bank reconciliation.

We recommended that:
Processing of vouchers should only be undertaken to those which were properly approved/signed and with complete supporting documentation;
The Municipal Treasurer submit to Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and
The Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended to Management that disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) must be done in accordance with the guidelines and accounting procedures set forth in the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to effectively monitor and determine whether or not the funds were utilized for its intended purpose and that expenses incurred were appropriately charged to fund. Disbursement of SEF through fund transfers/donations to schools must be stopped. Moreover, require the beneficiaries of CY 2018 to submit liquidation reports of the fund transfer.

We recommended that Management direct/instruct the Municipal Development Council to strictly include programs and projects chargeable to the 20 per cent Local Development Fund that are specifically embodied in the DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure the attainment of desirable social, economic and developmental outcomes for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that Management:
Create a GAD Focal Point System. This is through the Local Chief Executive signing appropriate issuances appointing LGU Officials to become part of the said GFPS. This must be composed of an Executive Committee and a Technical Working Group. This is lengthily discussed in Section 4a of PCW- DILG –DBM- NEDA Joint Memo Circular No. 2013- 01;
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and DILG Regional Office V for appropriate review and approval. The corresponding Accomplishment Report must also be prepared and submitted; and
Follow the proper way of budgeting in the allocation of 5 per cent for Gender and Development transactions.

We recommended that Management:
Require all drivers and concerned officials to completely fill out the Driver’s Trip ticket in accordance with existing regulation for transparency and accountability over the use of such government motor vehicles;
Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report of LGU vehicles and heavy equipment to the Commission on Audit (thru Municipal Accountant) for verification and evaluation of the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month; and
Comply with the lacking supporting documents of all the enumerated transactions. The Municipal Accountant must be cautious and strict in terms of compliance to complete documentary requirements per government contract.

We recommended that Management:
Construct a sanitary landfill and stop the use of an open controlled dumpsite. Segregation policy must be imposed upon collection of garbage. It is highly suggested that wastes/garbage which are not segregated at source must not be collected by garbage collector;
Prioritize implementation of Solid Waste Management programs and activities; and
Exert continuous effort in educating the people of Monreal the importance of Solid Waste Management.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer of the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account to ensure that the unspent balance are readily available for relief and recovery projects and activities within the next five (5) years; and
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare the monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format which must be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant. The same must be submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management adhere to the above cited implementing laws, rules and regulations pertinent to appropriation/disbursement of government fund for religious purpose. Management must exercise prudence in the disbursement of fund whether the same is in compliance with existing rules and regulations. We also recommend that Management direct the officials concerned to attach all lacking documents of the purchase from Fort’z Samut Sari-Sari Store.

We recommended that Management stop paying for the hotel accommodation of other government agency personnel staying in Monreal, Masbate because the latter are also given hotel allowances as part of their travel expenses. Moreover it is reminded that the Agency exercise caution in the disbursement of fund by adhering to rules and regulations to avoid those Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant, or Unconscionable (IUEEU) expenditures.

We recommended that Management abide by the rules and regulations on the submission  of  the  copies  of  perfected  contracts  and  purchase  orders  and  their
complete supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS. The Audit Team emphasizes that this finding has been reiterated for several years now.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor without further demand;
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the applicable provisions in the procurement of drugs and medicines provided under RA 3720 as amended and Executive Order No. 49 and other pertinent laws; and
Instruct the Accounting Office to require adequate documentation on each transaction involving procurement of goods before payment is effected.

We recommended to:
See to it that the 20 per cent Development Fund be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and
Implement immediately the thirteen (13) programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance as mentioned in the preceding discussion.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Inventory Committee and the General Services Officer to conduct a complete count of the Municipality’s PPE and prepare the Report as provided by Section 57, Volume II of NGAs Manual and submit the same to the Local Chief Executive and Auditor;
Require the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to maintain records on all properties of the Agency; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain ledger cards of all properties. This must be reconciled with stock/property cards maintained by the GSO or Municipal Treasurer

We recommended to send demand letters and/or exhaust all possible legal actions to the former Municipal Treasurer Ms. Belmonte to settle all his unliquidated cash advances. In addition, we advised that the Honorable Municipal Mayor: (a) direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who fail to comply with the requirement; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and send written demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; (c) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances; and (d) take legal actions against previous employees with still unliquidated cash advances who are no longer in the government service.
Also, it is reminded that pursuant to Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 040676 dated June 17, 2004 as amended by CSC Resolution No. 1200103 dated January 12, 2012, the failure of an accountable officer to render an account in full within the periods prescribed and after formal demand shall constitute the administrative offense of Gross Neglect of Duty punishable by dismissal from the service for the first offense. Full liquidation/settlement/payment of the subject cash advance outside the given periods shall constitute the offense of Simple Neglect of Duty punishable by suspension from the service for one (1) month and one (1) day to six (6) months for the first offense, and dismissal from the service for the second offense.

We recommended that the Honerable Municipal Mayor: (a) direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who fail to comply with the requirement; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash advance and send written demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; (c) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances; and (d) take legal actions against previous employees with still unliquidated cash advance who are no longer in the government service.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed on sending demand letters to former Municipal Treasurer and Employees with unliquidated cash advances. However, those balances pertaining to the previous Municipal Treasurer are still in progress for settlement. Demand letters were already sent and legal actions were also in process.
observations.reason: Most of the balances pertaining to the previous Municipal Treasurer are still in progress for settlement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Revenue Collection Officers to strictly turnover all their collections intact and in full amount at the close of the business day to the Municipal Treasurer. Collections should be deposited intact to the bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer explained that it was due to sometimes the delay in the deposit resulting to the accumulation because of the location of the bank from the municipality.
observations.reason: The location of the bank situated from the municipality in Ticao Island resulted to the delay in the deposit. The Municipal Treasurer committed to deposit all collections intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Local Development Council (LDC) to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to obtain copies of formats on the preparation of the LDRRMFIP as well as Utilization Report. They added that the same will be submitted for review.
observations.reason: There was confusion on the deadlines between the monthly and the annual report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency strengthen more its tax collection Scheme by sending regularly, notice or demands to taxpayers and making personal follow-ups to delinquent ones. The Municipality should avail of the legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Section 256, 257, and 258 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Collection process will be strengthened according to the Municipal Mayor. The residents of rural areas, majority situated far, still are delinquent in their respective RPT and SET. Notices and demand letters will be sent regularly.
observations.reason: Residents of the municipality from rural areas, majority situated far, still are delinquent in their respective RPT and SET.

observations.recommendation: We recommended prompt remittance of the agency's collections intended for other LGUs as prescribed under existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply with the audit recommendation and to remit the shares of the agency's collections out of the RPT intended for other LGUs.
